title: lightly steamed malaysian rice bowl
description: savory, chewy, light malaysian dish featuring brandy and yuzu-cure, vegan and gluten-free, suitable for buddhist temples. served in a bamboo tray.

ingredients.name: brandi-infused water
ingredients.notes: use a ratio of 1 part brandy to 4 parts water, steep for at least 2 hours, then chill.

ingredients.name: yuzu-cured tofu
ingredients.notes: marinate tofu in a mixture of yuzu juice, soy sauce, and maple syrup for at least 2 hours, then chill.

instructions: combine brandi-infused water and rice in a bamboo steamer and steam for 10 minutes.
meanwhile, toast coconut flakes in a small pan over low heat, stirring frequently, until lightly browned.
prepare yuzu-cured tofu by slicing into thin strips and placing on a plate.
in a small bowl, whisk together soy sauce, maple syrup, sesame oil, and grated ginger to make a sauce.
to assemble the dish, place a portion of steamed rice on a bamboo tray and top with yuzu-cured tofu, toasted coconut flakes, chopped scallions, and sesame oil sauce.
scatter salt and black pepper over the top and garnish with fresh cilantro, yuzu, and lemon wedges.
serve immediately, with additional yuzu wedges and lemon wedges on the side.
